programmes.show_name: Neil Sedaka Says: All You Need Is the Music
programmes.show_description: Hugely successful singer-songwriter Neil Sedaka plays hits from his early rock 'n' roll days and his later, more lyrical age in this special one-man show from the 1980s.

Janice Long and Simon Bates present the pop chart programme, first broadcast on 20 March 1986 and featuring Jim Diamond, Pet Shop Boys, The Real Thing, Diana Ross and Mr. Mister.

programmes.show_name: Poems in Their Place: WB Yeats
programmes.show_description: Seamus Heaney visits Coole Park, County Galway to read some of WB Yeats's work.

programmes.show_name: The Life and Loves of Oscar Wilde
programmes.show_description: A candid portrait of Oscar Wilde and his remarkable family, including revelations by his grandson Merlin Holland and Lady Alice Douglas.

programmes.show_name: The Picture of Dorian Gray: The Read with Luke Thompson
programmes.show_description: Oscar Wilde's haunting tale of beauty, vanity and moral corruption.
Donald Wolfit stars as Halvard Solness in 1958 adaption of Henrik Ibsen's play, set in Solness's Norwegian home in the late 19th century.
2/4. Mindfulness expert Andy Puddicombe shows what we can learn from the ways in which animals adapt to changes in the world around them.
